text:
Once upon a quiet town named Serenity Springs, every house had a secret garden. Mary was a girl born under a good luck star every March, and hers was vibrant with irises that opened to unveil baby birds each spring. She loved her garden so much that she could imagine anything growing there.

One summer, Mary invited her neighbors to come see her secret garden. There was Mr. Peterson, whose peonies were always vibrant pink, Mrs. Hawthorne’s apple tree bore golden apples ripe for picking, and little Polly the robin could perch on her shoulder and sing sweet songs.

In her garden, Mary dreamt of adventures. She imagined her own magic cloak that shimmered with starlight, transforming into a falcon to swoop through clouds. One night, she drew a tiny feather boa from her pillow and pretended to soar. Her friends laughed.

The next day, Mary discovered something extraordinary. While looking at her favorite flower—the rose she’d named Aurora—it felt as if someone had
